document_summary: Full text of the article 'New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS' by Dehuan Liu and Siluo Chen. Presents the ISMAS framework for mobile internet advertising, tracing the evolution from AIDMA to AISAS to the author-proposed ISMAS model, with the Nao Bai Jin case study.

snippets.text: # New Rules of Advertising Communication: From AIDMA, AISAS to ISMAS
snippets.subject: Article title as printed
snippets.reasoning: Title of Liu's published article proposing the ISMAS model.

snippets.subject: Authors as printed in the journal
snippets.reasoning: Confirms Liu as first author on the published page.

snippets.text: Television and print media, once filled with extensive advertising time and large advertising spaces, were labelled "traditional media" by the internet, and the emergence of the mobile internet turned the original internet into "traditional". The changes in the communication environment brought about changes in life, thereby promoting the transformation of marketing. From the AIDMA rule of the traditional mass media era to the AISAS rule of the traditional internet era, and now to the ISMAS rule proposed by the author for the mobile internet, the core of the consumer market has completely changed, and the marketing model is undergoing a Copernican- style revolution. Keeping a keen eye on changes in societal lifestyles, and staying highly sensitive to the details of consumer behavior patterns, is the basic respect that marketers owe to this ever- changing era.
snippets.subject: Description of ISMAS model proposed by Liu - a Copernican-style revolution in marketing
snippets.reasoning: Describes Liu's original contribution: the ISMAS rule for mobile internet marketing, described as a paradigm shift.
